California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 18, 1988


Michael D. Milich
Assistant City Attorney
City of Modesto
801 11th Street
P.O. Box 642
Modesto, CA 95353

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-247

Dear Mr. Milich:

We have received your letter concerning the new
restrictions on publicly-funded mass mailings under Proposition
73.

Enclosed is a copy of a second interim advice letter which
clarifies our June 16, 1988 advice. This second letter
provides that city newsletters and other mailings may be sent
at public expense so long as there is no reference to elected
city officials except in the standard letterhead or logotype.
This is only interim advice and may be changed in the future.

The Commission will address this and other questions
concerning the mass mailing restrictions in Proposition 73 at
its July 26, 1988 meeting. We will include your name on our
mailing list for information on the subject of mass mailings.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[illegible]

By: Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel, Legal Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 29, 1988


Michael D. Milich
Assistant City Attorney
P.O. Box 642
Modesto, CA 95353

Re: 88-247

Dear Mr. Milich:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on June 28, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

CITY of MODESTO
Office of City Attorney:
(209) 577-5284

801 11th Street, P. O. Box 642, Modesto, CA 95353
[TDD (209) 526-9211 Hearing and Speech Impaired only]

June 27, 1988

Ms. Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Prohibited Mass Mailings Under Proposition 73

Dear Ms. Griffiths:

We have received and reviewed your recent advice letter dated June 16, 1988,
regarding mass mailings. While the advice letter clarified several areas of
concern, we remain confused as to others. Generally, this confusion stems
from what appears to be a conflict between items "g" and "i" on page 8 of your
June 16, 1988, advice letter.

Accordingly, we seek your advice with regard to the following matters:

     1.     Every two months the City of Modesto sends out utility bills to
all utility customers. Our understanding is that the bills themselves are not
prohibited mass mailings. However, for some time the City has also included
an informational insert with each utility bill. For your reference, the
November-December 1987 and the March-April 1988 inserts are enclosed. Since
these inserts are "reports containing items of general interest" which do not
contain any reference to any elected official by "use of his or her name,
signature or depiction in any photograph", these inserts appear to be
permissible mass mailings under item "g" set forth on page 8 of the advice
letter. On the other hand, these inserts could be construed to be prohibited
under item "i" set forth on page 8 of your advice letter because they may be
interpreted as "unsolicited" "newsletters" which are sent to 200 or more
members of the public.

Ms. Diane M. Griffiths
June 27, 1988
Page two

2. Enclosed is a copy of a brochure which was sent to every
household in the City of Modesto to publicize the grand opening of the Modesto
Centre Plaza in March of this year. Similar mass mailings are contemplated
for future events at the Modesto Centre Plaza. Would such mass mailings be
allowed under Item "g" as a "report containing items of general interest" so
long as there is no reference to any elected official "by use of his or her
name, signature or depiction in any photograph" or would such a mass mailing
be prohibited under Item "i" as an "unsolicited" "substantially similar
correspondence sent to 200 or more constituents or other members of the
public"?

3. Periodically the City of Modesto through a committee appointed
by the City Council will conduct a survey to determine community concerns. A
copy of such a survey that was developed by the Forward Modesto IV Committee
and which was sent out in the Fall of 1986 is enclosed. Are future such
surveys prohibited under paragraph "i" of page 8 of the advice letter because
they are "unsolicited" "substantially similar correspondence sent to 200 or
more constituents or members of the public"?

4. Enclosed is a copy of the Modesto Police Department's
Neighborhood Watch Newsletter. Once again, this report appears to be
permitted under paragraph "g" of page 8 of the advice letter because it is a
"report containing items of general interest" which is not being sent by an
elected official or contain "any reference to an elected official by use of
his or her name, signature or depiction in any photograph". On the other
hand, the newsletter is routinely sent to 200 or more members of the public.
It is unsolicited and therefore appears to be prohibited by paragraph "i" of
page 8 of your advice letter.

5. Enclosed is a copy the City of Modesto's Report to the People
for the year 1988. The report was delivered to the City on June 16, 1988, by
the printer. It was to be mailed to 500 people on Friday, June 17, 1988. The
remaining 2,000 copies were to be advertised as available to the public. It
appears to us that the report would now be prohibited under paragraph "g" of
page 8 of your advice letter because it does contain the following: the
signature of the Mayor, a photograph of the entire City Council, and the names
of the Councilmembers. However, our question is whether or not this report
could be disseminated even after the photograph of the City Council and the
signature of the Mayor, and the names of the City Councilmembers were removed
from the report. Once again, it appears that upon making these deletions the
report would be a permissible mass mailing under paragraph "g" of page 8 of
your advice letter but prohibited under paragraph "i" of page 8 of your advice
letter because it is an "unsolicited" "substantially similar correspondence
sent to 200 or more constituents or other members of the public".

Ms. Diane M. Griffiths
June 27, 1988
Page three

6. Enclosed is the 1988 Summer Activity Guide. The last page of
the Guide contains a photograph of the Mayor and the names of the entire City
Council as well as the Mayor's signature. It is our understanding that these
portions of the Guide would have to be deleted for it to be a permissible mass
mailing under paragraph "i" of page 8 of your advice letter. As before, our
question is whether or not the Activity Guide would be prohibited under
paragraph "i" of page 8 of the newsletter because it is an "unsolicited"
"substantially similar correspondence sent to 200 or more constituents or
other members of the public". The Activity Guide is distributed once a year
by inserting it into the Modesto Bee newspaper which is then either mailed or
hand delivered to subscribers in the City of Modesto.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

MICHAEL D. MILICH
Assistant City Attorney

LOW INTEREST LOANS
NOW AVAILABLE

Small business owners in Modesto may be eligible
for low interest loans from the Economic Development Revolving Loan Fund (RLF) established
by the City of Modesto in conjunction with the State ELF and CDBG program.

The purpose of the RLF is to create or save jobs
for low-income Modesto residents, or to assist in
providing a needed service to a low income area of the
city.

Applicants for a loan may be a sole proprietorship, partnership, or for-profit corporation with a
net worth of less than $250,000. Eligible uses of
the loan include property acquisition, building construction,
renovation, expansion, capital equipment, interim
financing, working capital or venture capital, or a Small
Business Administration 504 Companion Loan
Program loan. The RLF can also be used for refinancing, for speculative building and renovation, or for
easements and projects not related to direct job
creation or retention.

Generally, the interest rate will be near the current bank prime rate. The RLF can provide up to $50,000 as a companion loan to $25,000
a single loan, with an equity share from the
applicant.

Business owners may submit applications at the
Economic Development Office, 401 H Street, Modesto.
A brief application will be sent to the Stanislaus
County Economic Development Corporation
for a recommendation to the City's Economic
Development Loan Fund Subcommittee, which will
review and approve each loan and set the terms.

For more information, please call 577-5245.

FREE CURBSIDE
RECYCLING
SERVICE -
EVERY
WEEK

Enjoy the convenience of weekly curbside recycled
material pick-up. This free service will end curbside to
your home.

Place your recyclables in separate bags or boxes
in a visible location at the curb front of your home
by 6 a.m. on your pick-up day. Boxes or other containers used for recyclables will be left for future
use. If you have questions about what your
customer, your recycling pick up day is the same as
your regular refuse collection. If you are a Modesto
Disposal Service customer, call Citizens Information
Services at 577-5454 to find out your pick-up day
and any changes that may affect you. Pick-up days
for all residents will be moved to the same day as
refuse collection in the near future. Those affected
will be notified when the change occurs.

Recycling is easy; just prepare your recyclables as
follows:

* NEWSPAPER - place in grocery sacks, boxes,
or tie in bundles.
* CARDBOARD - flattened, 4 ft. no longer than
and lots and staples
* ALUMINUM AND TIN - nine out, labels
* GLASS - place in plastic gallon jugs and
bags (no lids permitted on jars)
* MOTOR OIL - place in plastic gallon jugs and
bags (motor oil containers will not be
returned)

If you have other questions or comments about
recycling, call Robert Van Ostove, recycling program
coordinator, at 577-5491.

SANTA CLAUS IS COMING TO TOWN

The City of Modesto Parks and Recreation Department will
have a Santa Claus available for rental by children's groups
and children's parties from December 1 through December 23, 1987.
For reservations contact the Modesto Parks and
Recreation Office, 801 11th Street, starting November 16,
1987.

Rentals are available to City of Modesto residents only. Fees
for this unique holiday program are $12 per half hour between
9 a.m. and 5 p.m.; $15 per half hour after 5 p.m. Reservations
are limited to one per season per individual or group. Reser-
vations must be made two working days in advance of the
rental and are done on a first-come, first-served basis.
For more information about this unique service, please
call 577-5344.

HOLIDAY GARBAGE SCHEDULE

"When else will my garbage be picked up if it's a
holiday?"

Good question! With Thanksgiving, Christmas
and New Year's around the corner, you may
want to make a notation of the following informa-
tion on your calendar.

Modesto Garbage Disposal and Orange Line San
tation companies will pick up their garbage on their
regularly scheduled day even though it is a holiday
day. However, drivers will start much earlier than
normal so it would be a good idea to place your gar-
bage can out the previous night.

Those residences served by Gilton Solid
Waste will have their garbage picked up one
a day later than normal. The company will work
Saturdays during the week so holiday refuse
to catch up. Please allow an extra day for garbage
pickup before calling the City about a missed
pickup.

If you are unsure of which garbage company ser-
vices your neighborhood, please check the front of
your hill for that information or call 577-5434.

CONSERVATION TIPS FOR YOUR HOME

Water conservation is everyone's responsibility. Here are a few tips to help you save water at home. Can you think of others?

- Limit your shower time. Showers use 5-10 gallons of water per minute.
- Use your washbasin, rather than the toilet, for disposing of small items like facial tissue and cotton swabs. Each time you flush, 3-7 gallons of water are used.
- Wash your dishes or vegetables in a basin or cup for rinsing rather than running water.
- Pool water in the sink, rather than letting it run, when shaving or washing. Shaving with the tap running uses 20 gallons of water.
- Run your dishwasher and clothes washer only when you have a full load.
- Keep a bottle of water in the refrigerator for drinking.
- Fix faucet leaks. Even a small leak can waste 7 gallons a day.

Do you have a good conservation tip or need a question answered? Call us at 577-5469.

YOUR CITY
ON THE AIR

The City of Modesto, in cooperation with Pod Newsweek Cable, wants to provide you with as much information as possible about services, activities, special programs and events.

You can watch the City Council meetings live every Tuesday and at 7:30 p.m. on the second and fourth Tuesdays of each month.

The Modesto Planning Commission meetings are also broadcast live at 7:30 p.m. on the first and third Mondays of each month.

At 7 p.m. every Thursday, you can tune in to Channel 2 and see the Police Department's "Community Alert."

During the second and fourth weeks of each month, Cable Channel 2 broadcasts "Cityscape," a 30-minute show featuring a variety of City services, activities and programs. Hosted by Modesto's public information officer, "Cityscape" is broadcast at 6:30 p.m. Mondays, 4:30 p.m. Wednesdays and 12:30 p.m. Saturdays during the second and fourth weeks of the month.

MODESTO CENTRE PLAZA
OPEN FOR PUBLIC USE

Modesto Centre Plaza is now open for public use.

What was once a dream talked about before World War II and in each decade thereafter, is now reality. The plaza will continue to be developed for the remainder of this year — everything from high school proms to private wedding receptions and business meetings.

The Centre staff is eager to work with you in planning your special event. The new building located at 1000 L Street. The Centre has space for 20 to 2,000. Organizations or an division interested in using Centre facilities should call 577-5497 for information on available dates and rental policies.

WHAT ABOUT ROCKWELLS?

Although 70% of Modestans live in areas drained by rockwell, most of us know little about them. In fact, most of us are not aware of them until the rains come — and the streets flood. The following information may help you keep your neighborhood rockwell operating more satisfactorily.

Q: What is a rockwell?
A rockwell is a hole that is excavated into the ground and filled with graded rock. It is usually located in an area where storm waters are likely to collect. When stormwater collects in the area surrounding the rockwell, water enters the spaces between the particles of rock. The width and depth of the hole usually provide enough surface area for the water to be absorbed by the surrounding soil.

Q: Why does the street always flood during the rains?
Since rockwells have a limited storage capacity and fairly low absorption rates, ponding will occur in the street or intersection when rainfall exceeds 0.25 inches during a storm. Analysis of Modesto rainfall shows that the City will exceed that amount 14 times during a year. Therefore, ponding can be expected during heavy rains, and residents in flood-prone areas should not be unduly concerned if the area drains within 24 to 36 hours after a storm.

Q: What can I do?
A rockwell can only be efficient if it washes are clear of obstructions; therefore it's important that the area surrounding the rockwell be kept as clean as possible. City crews clean each rockwell on the average of once a year. You can help by keeping litter, grass clippings, motor oil, grease and other chemicals away from entrances to a rockwell. All of these items can clog a rockwell and reduce its "flow" look.

Q: Who do I call with a question or concern about a rockwell?
If you have additional questions concerning the operation of a rockwell, please don't hesitate to telephone Citizens Information Services at 577-5254.

Season's
Greetings

HOLIDAY SAFETY TIPS

The Modesto Fire Department would like to offer some safety tips to home that you and your family have a safe and happy holiday season.

• Keep your fresh by watering it regularly and remove it from the house before its needles dry out.

• Be careful when you experiment the use of candles, but if you must use them, keep candles away from the tree, decorations, curtains and other flammable materials.

• Remember to turn off all tree lights, extinguish all candles and check chimneys before you go to bed at night.

• Dispose of wrapping paper in the trash — never in a fireplace.

• Make sure your fireplace screen is closed when the fireplace is in use. Keep decorations and other combustibles away from heaters.

• Check your tree lights prior to putting them on the tree to see that they are in good condition.

If you have someone on your gift list and you don't know what to give them, you might consider giving them a gift subscription to a favorite magazine or gift that say "you really care. This one simple gift could save a life.

If you have any questions or concerns about fire prevention, please call 577-5412.

A GIFT IDEA FOR THE

The McHenry Mansion, Modesto's most elegant Victorian house, presents a unique gift idea. The book — with its many photographs and text about how closed McHenry's and their house were instrumental in the development of our City — makes an excellent gift.

The McHenry Mansion Foundation, a non-profit organization founded in 1983 to assure preservation of the Mansion to aged by the City).

CHRISTMAS
TREE PICKUP

After Christmas, the City will pick up Christmas trees at the curb where your regular garbage cans are placed. The trees should be cut if longer than eight feet and take any tree thicker than 3 inches in diameter. If you have additional questions or Christmas tree pickup, please call [illegible].

VOL. I        Modesto Police Department — Crime Prevention Unit        MARCH 1988

NEW ACCIDENT INVESTIGATION POLICY

Starting in February, 1988, the Modesto
Police Department is changing its
accident investigation policy on
property-damage-only motor vehicle
collisions. Police officers will:

1. Still respond to the scene of an
   accident.
2. We will insure that the roadway is
   clear and direct traffic as necessary.
3. We will obtain tow trucks for those
   parties needing them.
4. We will determine if any of the
   following violations have occurred:
   a. Driving under the influence.
   b. Driver's license violations.
   c. Proof of insurance violations.
   d. If any of the parties are injured.

If the accident meets the above criteria,
we will supply each driver a card to
complete which includes name, address,
and insurance company. We will remain at
the scene until all drivers have completed
and exchanged those cards.

We will not investigate the cause of the
accident and will only report the
drivers' last names, vehicle license
numbers, and type of collision (that is
whether it is a broadside, rearend, etc.)
and the specific block number where the
accident occurred.

If when the officer is at the scene of
the collision he determines that either
of the drivers are in violation of Number
4 above, a complete investigation will be
done of the accident.

Our change in policy became necessary to
increase the amount of time officers will
be available for major criminal
investigations.

CRIME'S GOING DOWN!

In 1987 The City of Modesto experienced a
dramatic reduction of reported property
crimes. The most significant reduction
was in the area of residential
burglaries. Reported residential
burglaries were down 23% over the
reported burglaries in 1986.

In February 1987, the Property Crimes
Detail of the Modesto Police Department
(which has the responsibility of
investigating burglaries and grand
thefts) under went a major change of
direction. The investigations went from
a reactive mode of investigation
(investigating "cold burglaries") to a
more proactive type of investigation.
The new direction of investigation was
focused on narcotics violations.

Historically it has been proven that the
property crimes violators are for the
most part also narcotics violators.
Therefore, putting the narcotics
violators in jail will reduce the
reported property crimes.

As a result of the change in tactics, the
following data was collected.

In 1987 there were 4,544 cases assigned
to the Property Crimes Detail. This
number is down considerably compared to
1986, where 5,208 cases were assigned.

In 1987 Detectives in the Property Crimes
Detail made 427 arrests for property and
narcotics violations. They obtained and
served 130 search warrants. As a result
of these arrests and search warrants,
$263,752 worth of stolen property was
recovered. Also, $49,000 worth of
illegal drugs and narcotics were seized.

As a result of the change in direction of
the Property Crimes Detail and efforts of
the Field Operations Division and their
Tactical Patrol Unit, Property Crimes in
the City of Modesto are down and we hope
that this trend will continue throughout
1988.

NEW TRAFFIC LAWS

There is new legislation concerning
traffic laws that affect us all. Three
new or amended laws are:

1. 22507.8 CVC (California Vehicle
Code): This bill adds to the
existing handicapped parking laws.
It now illegal to obstruct, block or
otherwise bar access to disabled
parking stalls. This applies to
public and private owned lots

2. 23117 CVC-Tethered Dogs: This is a
new section which prohibits the
transporting of a dog in the back of
a truck or a space designed for a
load unless:

* The truck has racks 46 inches high,
or
* The truck bed is enclosed, or
* The dog is cross tethered, or
* The dog is in a cage.

This section does not apply to:

* Transportation of livestock, or
* Dogs being transported for the
purpose of ranching or farming.

For information on how to set up the
tethering system contact the local SPCA
office.

3. 22526 CVC-Anti-Gridlock: This is a
new section which makes it a
violation for a vehicle to enter an
intersection on a yellow or a green
traffic light when there is no
possibility for that vehicle to
completely clear the intersection
prior to the light turning red.

The bill designates that if the
intersection is posted the driver will
receive a citation for failure to obey a
traffic sign. If the intersection is not
posted the driver will be cited for a
parking violation.

During heavy rush hour traffic, be sure
you can completely cross the intersection
or you might receive a citation if your
car is unable to clear the intersection
when the traffic light turns red.

CHECK FRAUD UPDATE

In our September 1987 SCAN, we mentioned
that the Police Department was going to
start a new forgery investigation
policy. We wanted to update you on the
results to date.

In October 1987, the Modesto Police
Department instituted a policy of not
investigating any fraudulent payroll or
Government checks if the merchant had not
obtained the right thumbprint of the
person cashing the check. The policy was
instituted because of the large volume of
fraudulent checks that could not be
prosecuted because of lack of positive
identification of the passer.

Since the thumbprint policy was
instituted, the Police Department has
experienced a dramatic reduction in the
number of reported forgeries involving
payroll or Government checks.

By July 1st of this year, the Police
Department intends to extend the
thumbprint policy to all fraudulent check
cases.

Help prevent forgery by requiring the
person who gives you a check to place
their right thumbprint on the check. A
thumbprint is positive identification
that a criminal will not want to give you.

ARE YOU COVERED?

On October 27, 1987, the Supreme Court of
California reviewed the financial
responsibility laws of the California
Vehicle Code passed in July, 1985. In
the Supreme Court's review they held that
Section 16028 CVC was constitutional and
would be enforceable starting November
27, 1987.

We are now citing those drivers stopped
for other violations when they cannot
show proof of insurance or proof of
financial responsibility.

What does proof of financial
responsibility and proof of insurance
mean? It means drivers of vehicles
should have in their possession their
insurance company name and policy number
or a Surety bond number. This
information can be written on the
registration card in the space provided.

sections.question:
is whether or not the Activity Guide would be prohibited under 
paragraph "i" of page 8 of the newsletter because it is an "unsolicited" 
"substantially similar correspondence sent to 200 or more constituents or 
other members of the public". 
The Activity Guide is distributed once a year 
by inserting it into the Modesto Bee newspaper which is then either mailed or 
hand delivered to subscribers in the City of Modesto.

QUESTION: is whether or not the Activity Guide would be prohibited under 
paragraph "i" of page 8 of the newsletter because it is an "unsolicited" 
"substantially similar correspondence sent to 200 or more constituents or 
other members of the public". 
The Activity Guide is distributed once a year 
by inserting it into the Modesto Bee newspaper which is then either mailed or 
hand delivered to subscribers in the City of Modesto.

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission July 18, 1988 Michael D. Milich Assistant City Attorney City of Modesto 801 11th Street P.O. Box 642 Modesto, CA 95353 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. I-88-247 Dear Mr. Milich: We have received your letter concerning the new restrictions on publicly-funded mass mailings under Proposition 73. Enclosed is a copy of a second interim advice letter which clarifies our June 16, 1988 advice. This second letter provides that city newsletters and other mailings may be sent at public expense so long as there is no reference to elected city officials except in the standard letterhead or logotype. This is only interim advice and may be changed in the future. The Commission will address this and other questions concerning the mass mailing restrictions in Proposition 73 at its July 26, 1988 meeting. We will include your name on our mailing list for information on the subject of mass mailings. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel [illegible] By: Kathryn E. Donovan Counsel, Legal Division DMG:KED:plh Enclosure 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 California Fair Political Practices Commission June 29, 1988 Michael D. Milich Assistant City Attorney P.O. Box 642 Modesto, CA 95353 Re: 88-247 Dear Mr. Milich: Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on June 28, 1988 by the Fair Political Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to information needed. If your request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) You also should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. Very truly yours, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel DMG:plh 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 CITY of MODESTO Office of City Attorney: (209) 577-5284 801 11th Street, P. O. Box 642, Modesto, CA 95353 [TDD (209) 526-9211 Hearing and Speech Impaired only] June 27, 1988 Ms. Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 P. O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804-0807 Re: Prohibited Mass Mailings Under Proposition 73 Dear Ms. Griffiths: We have received and reviewed your recent advice letter dated June 16, 1988, regarding mass mailings.
